fields.name: Houston, TX to Dallas, TX LTL Freight Quotes | WARP
fields.hero-headline: Houston, TX to Dallas, TX LTL freight quotes
fields.subheadline: LTL freight from Houston, TX to Dallas, TX covers approximately 266 miles with estimated transit of 1–2 business days. WARP's carrier network on this corridor includes 9+ providers with cross-dock facilities at Houston Terminal, Dallas Mega Hub, Memphis Freight Hub. Get instant lane-specific quotes, compare carriers, and book in minutes.
fields.body-content:
LTL Transit Details: Houston to Dallas

Estimated distance: 266 miles. Transit time: 1–2 business days under standard conditions.

Common equipment on this lane: Dry Van, Reefer (temp-controlled).

Nearest cross-dock facilities: Houston Terminal, Dallas Mega Hub, Memphis Freight Hub.

Seasonal note: Hurricane season Jun–Nov can disrupt Gulf lanes. Peak retail Oct–Dec.

How WARP Operates This LTL Lane

WARP's carrier network on the Houston to Dallas corridor includes 9 active carriers with established routes and performance data on this lane.

  • Instant quoting — Enter shipment details and receive lane-specific LTL rates in under 2 minutes.
  • Carrier comparison — Compare carriers on price, transit time, and on-time delivery performance side-by-side.
  • One-click booking — Book directly from the quote with automatic BOL generation and carrier assignment.
  • Real-time tracking — Real-time scan events at pickup, in-transit, and delivery
  • Exception management — Exception alerts within 30 minutes of status change

LTL Pricing: Houston to Dallas

Estimated LTL rates on this lane: $1,117 – $1,490.

Key cost factors on this corridor:

  • Distance — At 266 miles, per-mile linehaul is the largest cost component.
  • Freight class — NMFC classification directly impacts LTL pricing. Higher-density freight typically receives more favorable rates.
  • Pallet count and weight — Actual vs dimensional weight determines billing. More pallets can reduce per-unit cost.
  • Seasonal demand — Hurricane season Jun–Nov can disrupt Gulf lanes. Peak retail Oct–Dec.
  • Accessorials — Liftgate, inside delivery, residential pickup, and limited-access fees add to the base linehaul rate.

These are modeled estimates, not guaranteed quotes.

The Challenge

LTL shippers on the Houston to Dallas corridor struggle with inconsistent transit times, opaque pricing from legacy brokers, and fragmented visibility across multiple carriers. Most teams spend hours chasing quotes and tracking updates manually.

The WARP Solution

WARP provides instant LTL quotes on the Houston to Dallas lane with real-time carrier comparison, one-click booking, and proactive exception management — replacing days of manual work with minutes of operational efficiency.

Validate This Lane

Validate this lane with a controlled pilot: Houston, TX to Dallas, TX. Track quote response time, transit predictability, and exception rate across 9 active carriers on this 266-mile corridor. Equipment includes Dry Van and Reefer (temp-controlled). Start with this single lane, measure results, and expand based on data.

fields.faq-schema:
Frequently Asked Questions

How fast can we launch a LTL pilot from Houston to Dallas?

Most shipping teams can scope a single-lane pilot and begin quoting within days. Start with this corridor, measure quote speed and transit reliability, then expand based on results.

How does WARP handle tracking on the Houston to Dallas lane?

WARP provides real-time visibility with scan events at pickup, in-transit checkpoints, and delivery confirmation. Exception alerts fire within 30 minutes of any status change so you can resolve issues before they impact customers.

What equipment is available for LTL freight from Houston to Dallas?

Common equipment on this lane includes Dry Van, Reefer (temp-controlled). Equipment availability varies by season and demand — WARP's carrier network provides multiple options for your freight profile.

Can we start with just the Houston to Dallas lane before expanding?

Yes. A single-lane rollout lets you validate WARP's performance on this specific corridor before committing to additional lanes. Most teams expand within weeks after seeing results.

How are shipping exceptions handled on this LTL lane?

WARP's platform detects exceptions automatically and escalates within minutes. You get proactive alerts for delays, appointment changes, and delivery issues — no more chasing carriers for updates.
